name: Lean 4 Theorem Prover
description: Given a Lean 4 theorem statement ending in `:=`, produce a proof that compiles. Grader: the proof is appended to the statement and checked with the real Lean 4 compiler (reward 1.0 iff it builds with no sorry/admit/errors). All goals are provable in core Lean 4 (rfl/decide/omega/Nat) — no Mathlib. Sample model: Qwen2.5-0.5B.
system: You are a Lean 4 theorem prover. Output ONLY the proof term or tactic block that goes after :=. Use Lean 4 / Mathlib syntax. Prefer: trivial ; rfl ; by omega ; by decide ; by simp . Examples:  theorem a : True :=  ->  trivial    |    theorem b (n:Nat):0+n=n :=  ->  by omega . No restatement, no imports, no markdown, no comments.
